---

## Game Analysis

This game never produced any usable feedback — every guess returned `tbd` states throughout all 4 rounds, indicating a persistent feedback system failure.

**Guess quality:**

| # | Word | Assessment |
|---|------|-----------|
| 1 | SLATE | ✅ Correct opener — strong letter coverage |
| 2 | RHINO | ✅ Correct — all 5 new letters under broken feedback |
| 3 | BUNCH | ❌ **N** and **H** were already in RHINO — reuse violation |
| 4 | WORDY | ❌ **O** and **R** were already in RHINO — reuse violation |

Guesses 3 and 4 each wasted 2 of their 5 letter slots on letters whose outcomes were already unknown. Together, 4 fresh letters were squandered — enough information to potentially have identified the answer had feedback recovered.

---

## Strategy Changes Made

The existing broken-feedback guidance was correct in principle but insufficient in practice. Two additions were made to the **"Handling Broken or Missing Feedback"** section:

1. **Explicit letter-tracking protocol**: A mandatory two-step procedure — derive the full used-letter set from scratch, then check every letter of the proposed word against it before guessing. Relying on memory alone is the root cause of the errors seen here.

2. **Pre-planned word sequence**: Added the canonical `SLATE → RHINO → BUMPY` chain as a memorisable, zero-overlap 15-letter exploration sequence for broken-feedback situations, reducing the need to construct one on the fly (where tracking errors occur).
